era_context: Identical to -1630: Late Bronze Age, 18th Dynasty Egypt, Hittite consolidation, Shang Dynasty China. This year falls within the same broad historical period and likely the same reigns as -1630.

events.title: Continuation of 18th Dynasty rule in Egypt
events.description: The 18th Dynasty continues its imperial administration. This year likely falls within the reign of Thutmose III or his successor. The pharaonic state maintains control over Nubia, the Levant, and the Nile Valley through a combination of military force, diplomatic marriages, and administrative bureaucracy. Temple construction and maintenance continue as expressions of royal power and religious devotion.

sources.name: Egyptian temple inscriptions and administrative papyri

events.title: Hittite military and diplomatic activities
events.description: The Hittite Empire continues its consolidation and expansion. Military campaigns against neighboring kingdoms and diplomatic negotiations with regional powers (Egypt, Mitanni, Assyria) are ongoing. The Hittites are developing their characteristic military organization and legal codes during this period.

sources.name: Hittite royal inscriptions and diplomatic correspondence

events.title: Shang Dynasty oracle bone divination and royal hunts
events.description: The Shang Dynasty continues its characteristic practices of oracle bone divination and royal hunting expeditions. These activities are recorded on oracle bones (jiaguwen), which provide glimpses into the concerns of the Shang court: weather, harvests, military campaigns, and the will of ancestors and spirits.
events.certainty_note: Same chronological uncertainty as -1630; Shang chronology remains debated.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for -1631 distinct from -1630. The same chronological debates apply.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1631 is essentially identical to -1630: sparse, regionally fragmented, and heavily dependent on palace and temple sources. Individual years in this period are difficult to distinguish archaeologically or textually; the significance of -1631 versus -1630 is minimal.
